texto: You must think I don’t know what’s keeping you up at night. You must think we’re so different. But I know. Everyone knows despair. Isn’t that why people jump off bridges or drown themselves? Anything, really, to escape being human.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de V.J. Campilan que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “You must think I don’t know what’s keeping you up at night. You must think we’re so different. Bu…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, despair, think, com foco em boa-noite e reflexao-noturna.

frase_original: You must think I don’t know what’s keeping you up at night. You must think we’re so different. But I know. Everyone knows despair. Isn’t that why people jump off bridges or drown themselves? Anything, really, to escape being human.

texto: Do you know that you being here with that gun in your hand is more wondrous than the parting of the Red Sea? For someone like God, it would be easy to command the skies, the waters, the rocks. But to change a man’s heart, for a man to choose to come here and acknowledge his helplessness, now, that is the province of miracles.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a V.J. Campilan: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “Do you know that you being here with that gun in your hand is more wondrous than the parting of t…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, miracles, being pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de mudanca e reflexao.

frase_original: Do you know that you being here with that gun in your hand is more wondrous than the parting of the Red Sea? For someone like God, it would be easy to command the skies, the waters, the rocks. But to change a man’s heart, for a man to choose to come here and acknowledge his helplessness, now, that is the province of miracles.

texto: You’re trying to look for rock bottom, to that part of yourself that could no longer feel pain. But there is no such thing as rock bottom. As long as there is left to destroy in you, you’d do it. We always feel the need to sink ourselves because we keep being intolerable, because if we’re suffering then maybe people would give us a break for all the shameful things we do. You think you could impose your own penance, but it never goes away, does it? That kind of deadening that’s worse than actual dying.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de V.J. Campilan que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “You’re trying to look for rock bottom, to that part of yourself that could no longer feel pain. B…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, despair, pain, com foco em pain.

texto: Why come back to this empty house, and this Manila with a strange face; the one I never knew? All those lonely islands. They will keep afloat without me.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de V.J. Campilan que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “Why come back to this empty house, and this Manila with a strange face; the one I never knew? All…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, homecoming, hometown, com foco em hometown e lonely.
